StudyActivityEditForm.flowchart_group: Select the SoA group the activity/activities should be assigned to

StudyStatus.core_attributes_body:
Specification of the core attributes for a study, being relationship to clinical programme and project, study number (will be linked to the Study ID) and the Study Acronym.

When a study have not yet been locked it can be deleted from this page, this will be a soft delete and the study will be listed under Deleted studies on the Study List menu.
Note the history displayed from this page is actually the overall history for the study definition.
StudyStatus.content_line_1: A study definition instance can have the following status:
StudyStatus.content_line_2: Draft, refer to the current instance that can be edited.
StudyStatus.content_line_3: Released, refer to a minor version of a stable instance (snapshot) for downstream testing and review.
StudyStatus.content_line_4: Locked, refer to a major version of a stable instance (snapshot) for downstream usage in final deliverables. As this is a major version several constraint checks must be fulfilled for a study definition instance can be locked. Both a locked version instance and a released instance is made when the study is locked to avoid the latest released is older than the latest locked.
StudyStatus.content_line_5: The page will list the available study definition instances and their status.
StudyStatus.content_line_6: The possible actions depend on the current state and status of the study.
StudyStatus.content_line_7: When study is in Draft status the Release action is enabled
StudyStatus.content_line_8:
The possible actions depend on the current state and status of the study.
This will create a stable persistent study instance that can be referred to for downstream usage, as a data snapshot reference in the repository.
A concurrent minor version number will automatically be assigned (0.1, 0.2, …, 1.1, 1.2, …).
The study will continue to be in 'Draft' status.
Release is like check-in in a document management system or a commit and push in a Git repository.
StudyStatus.content_line_9: When study is in Draft status the Lock action is enabled
StudyStatus.content_line_10:
This will create a versioned stable persistent study instance that can be referred to from downstream usage, as a data snapshot reference in the repository. The study will change status to 'Locked' and can no longer be edited. To continue to edit, an un-lock action must be done starting up a new version.
A concurrent major version number will automatically be assigned (1.0, 2.0, 3.0, …).
The locking process will also make a study release instance to ensure the latest release instance never is older than the latest locked instance.
Lock and Release is like creating a final approved version in a document management system or a merging a pull request into main in a Git repository.
StudyStatus.content_line_11: When study is in Locked status the Un-lock action is enabled
StudyStatus.content_line_12: This will bring the study back into the 'Draft' status where the study again can be edited.
StudyStatus.study_sub_parts_body: Specification for the study definition as a parent definition for several sub-study definitions, or as a sub-study definition under a parent. This is the approach for making a study specification for protocols holding multiple sub-study parts.
StudyStatus.protocol_version_body: Specification of the relationship between versions of study definition instances and the corresponding protocol document versions.

FootnotesTemplatesTable.general: Select from the table of templates. Either from generic templates or pre-specified templates, by using the 'Select from pre-instance'-slide in the top of the table.

FootnotesTable.general: List of footnotes applicable for the Schedule of Activities (SoA).
FootnotesTable.covered_items: The itmes in the SoA that the footnote is related to, e.g. a visits or an Activity.

StudyFootnoteForm.general: Footnotes are additional information pertaining to elements of the SoA. For instruction related to Activities, The Activity Instruction tab should be used.
StudyFootnoteForm.creation_mode_label: Select the type of method for creating a footnote: use a standard, from an existing study, or make one form scratch.

ProjectForm.general: Add a new project to a clinical programme.
ProjectForm.name: Name for the new project, example: 'Demo Project'.
ProjectForm.project_number: Project ID for the new project, example: 'AB1234'.
ProjectForm.description: Description of the project, example: 'Project for demonstration purposes'.
ProjectForm.clinical_programme: Clinical programme that the project belongs to.

ClinicalProgrammeForm.name: Name for the new clinical programme.

UsdmPage.general: This page display the JSON file generated based on the  Digital Data Flow (DDF) CDISC / TransCelerate Unified Study Definitions Model (USDM). The data available here are extracted and converted into the USDM model, based on mapping rules defined within the OpenStudyBuilder team: Be aware about that.
UsdmPage.official_protocol_title: The formal descriptive name for the protocol.
UsdmPage.clinical_study_def: A clinical study involves research using human volunteers (also called participants) that is intended to add to medical knowledge. There are two main types of clinical studies: clinical trials (also called interventional studies) and observational studies. [[http://ClinicalTrials.gov]](CDISC Glossary)
UsdmPage.study_protocol_version: A plan at a particular point in time for a formal investigation to assess the utility, impact, pharmacological, physiological, and/or psychological effects of a particular treatment, procedure, drug, device, biologic, food product, cosmetic, care plan, or subject characteristic. (BRIDG)
UsdmPage.protocol_status: A condition of the protocol at a point in time with respect to its state of readiness for implementation. See [C188723-CDISC DDF Protocol Status Value Set Terminology]
UsdmPage.study_design: A plan detailing how a study will be performed in order to represent the phenomenon under examination, to answer the research questions that have been asked, and informing the statistical approach.
UsdmPage.organization_type: A characterization or classification of the formalized group of persons or other organizations collected together for a common purpose (such as administrative, legal, political) and the infrastructure to carry out that purpose. See [C188724-CDISC DDF Organization Type Value Set Terminology]
UsdmPage.trial_type: The nature of the interventional study for which information is being collected. See [C66739-CDISC SDTM Trial Type Terminology]
UsdmPage.intervention_model_type: The general design of the strategy for assigning interventions to participants in a clinical study. (clinicaltrials.gov). See [C99076-CDISC SDTM Intervention Model Terminology]
UsdmPage.therapeutic_areas: A categorization of a disease, disorder, or other condition based on common characteristics and often associated with a medical specialty focusing on research and development of specific therapeutic interventions for the purpose of treatment and prevention.
UsdmPage.trial_blinding_schema: The type of experimental design used to describe the level of awareness of the study subjects and/ or study personnel as it relates to the respective intervention(s) or assessments being observed, received or administered. See [C66735-CDISC SDTM Trial Blinding Schema Terminology]
UsdmPage.target_study_population: The population within the general population to which the study results can be generalized.

StudyStructuresOverview.general: The Study Structures page is intended to assist Standards Developers by offering an overview of how existing trials have been defined. It allows users to view the study structures they can replicate, identify trends, and spot any deviations. The Study Structures page aims to increase consistency when setting up new trials. Note that studies that share the same structure are grouped together and listed in the table under the same row. The columns display numeric values indicating the number of arms/epochs/elements in the trial or Y/N to indicate if cohorts are part of the trial design.

ActivityInstanceForm.general: An activity instance is the data collection specification of an activity to be used to link the operational SoA with data collection instruments such as CRFs and external data specifications. The activity instance carries metadata for SDTM generation and links to ADaM through PARAM and PARAMCD.
ActivityInstanceForm.step1_description: Search for an activity to add an instance to. If more than one grouping exists make sure to choose the relevant one. If no relevant activity is present then cancel this flow and create a new activity.
ActivityInstanceForm.step2_description: Select an activity instance class and a data domain. These selections will determine the required and optional metadata to be defined for SDTM
ActivityInstanceForm.step3_description: Specify additional details to be used for the generation of PARAM and PARAMCD. The selection of values will autogenerate a suggestion for a unique name for the activity instance, PARAMCD and topic code. Its possible to manually modify the names.
ActivityInstanceForm.step4_description: Specify optional activity items that are not part of PARAM/PARAMCD but will be in SDTM. These values are used to specify data that can be collected on a CRF or a external specification. Examples are methods, anatomical locations or evaluator information that will increase the value of secondary use of data.

CohortsStepper.select_class: “Study with cohorts, branches and subpopulations” will bring up the full stepper, helping you set up cohorts and branches in an easy and controlled way. Study participants must be as-signed to each branch and are then automatically assigned to cohorts and arms as well. “Study with arms only” will only bring up the first page of the stepper and participants must be assigned to arms directly. It is still possible to add cohorts and branches manually after-wards.
CohortsStepper.set_arms_for_study: You can add as many arms as required in your study using the “Add arm” button.
CohortsStepper.study_arm_type: There are four options available for study arm type: Comparator arm, Investigational arm, Observational arm and Placebo arm.
CohortsStepper.arm_name: Arm name can be filled in with free text but is most commonly the name of the investigation, e.g. “Semaglutide” or “Placebo”.
CohortsStepper.arm_short_name: Arm short name can be filled in with free text but is most commonly the same as the arm name.
CohortsStepper.rand_group: Randomisation group should be filled out when known. Randomisation group must be filled out with capital letters (e.g. A). If multiple groups apply for one arm, they must be separated using pipes (e.g. AIBID).
CohortsStepper.arm_code: Arm code is generated automatically within Novo Nordisk but may be applicable for open-source users.
CohortsStepper.set_cohorts_for_study: You can add as many cohorts/subpop as required in your study using the “Add Co-horts/subpop.” button.
CohortsStepper.source_var: There are three options available for source variable: Cohort, Subgroup or Stratum.
CohortsStepper.cohort_code: Cohort code must be filled out with a numerical value (e.g. 1). Most common approach is numbering each cohort in numerical order.
CohortsStepper.cohort_name: Cohort/subpop. name can be filled in with free text but is most commonly the name of the investigation + dose level, e.g. “Semaglutide 2.4 mg” or “Placebo 2.4 mg”.
CohortsStepper.cohort_short_name: Cohort/subpop. short name can be filled in with free text but is most commonly the same as the cohort/subpop. name or sometimes just the dose level.
CohortsStepper.link_arms_cohorts: Enter the number of participants per branch. It is possible to copy the number of participants from the first row to all other rows. Branch name and branch short name is automatically generated based on the cohort and arm that the branch is assigned to. Using the Edit button, it is possible to rename the branch name and branch short name.

StudyDataSpecifications.general: The 'Data Specifications' page displays all selected study activities and allows linking each activity to its corresponding activity instances. Additionally for each activity instances the study supplier, importance or baseline flags may be defined (see 'Study Activity Instances' page). It can also visualize activities and their instances in the operational SoA view, showing their linkage with study visits (see 'Operational SoA' page).
StudyDataSpecifications.instances_actions:
This table lists study activities and their instances. Each row shows one activity linked to one activity instance. If an activity is linked to multiple instances, it will appear in multiple rows, each showing a different instance. The State/Actions column shows the state of an activity or activity instance, or the action expected to be taken. There are six possible states/actions:
1) Review not needed
2) Review needed
3) Reviewed
4) Add instance
5) Remove instance
6) Not applicable
'Review not needed' requires no action from the user. It is a state specific to a mandatory activity instance, meaning the instance is required for the activity and is not expected to be removed. When the 'Review not needed' state is shown for an activity-activity instance pair, the 'Reviewed' checkbox is automatically checked. The row with the 'Review needed' state requires attention from the user. The user should verify whether the activity-activity instance pair meets study requirements. The activity instance may need to be changed to a different instance, or the activity may need to be linked to another activity instance. After the necessary changes are made, the user can select the 'Reviewed' checkbox for each row carrying the 'Review needed' state, which will change the state to 'Reviewed'. 'Add instance' state/action is displayed for an activity row that is not linked to any activity instance. The 'Reviewed' checkbox cannot be selected in this situation, because the expected action is to first create the relationship between the activity and an instance. 'Remove instance' is shown for an activity for which multiple instances are not allowed. This means that such an activity can only be paired with a single instance at the study level. The expected action from the user is to remove the unwanted activity-activity instance pair by clicking the ellipsis icon and selecting Delete Activity Instance relationship. 'Not applicable' is a state for activities that are not expected to be linked to any instances and for which the data collection attribute is set to NO. No user action is required. The 'Reviewed' checkbox cannot be checked for activities that carry the 'Not applicable' state. Please note that for rows with the 'Reviewed' and 'Review not needed' states, the 'Reviewed' checkbox may be ticked off. Clearing it will change the state/action to 'Review needed'
StudyDataSpecifications.instances_ellipsis:
Use the ellipsis (three dots) to the left of an activity to access these options:
1) Edit Activity - Instance Relationship,
2) Delete Activity - Instance Relationship,
3) Update Instance to new version.
The Edit option allows changing the activity-activity instance relationship for an activity. A new relationship (activity instance) can also be added. When adding or editing an instance, the data supplier (including data origin and data type), importance and baseline flags can be specified for the selected instance. These changes can also be made via the table edit mode — to open the table in this mode, click the pencil icon. The Delete option removes unwanted activity-activity instance pairs from the table. The Update Instance to New Version option updates an instance to the latest version available in the OpenStudyBuilder library. After an activity instance used in your trial has been updated in the library, you will be notified by a red exclamation-mark icon displayed to the left of the activity row. In that situation, the Update Instance to New Version option becomes available; otherwise, this option is not availa-ble. If the user decides to update the instance to the latest version, the red exclamation-mark icon will disappear. If the update is declined and the instance is kept as is, the red icon will be replaced by a yellow exclamation-mark icon.
StudyDataSpecifications.instances_exclamation: All, Red and Yellow exclamation-mark buttons are placed in the middle of the page just above the table. By default, the table is set to show all instances — the All button is turned on. However, the user may want to filter the table to see which study instances have been updated in the library to a new version. To do that, select the button with the red exclamation-mark. To filter the table to see instances that were updated in the library but for which the user declined the update (instances kept as they are), select the button with the yellow exclamation-mark.
StudyDataSpecifications.instances_updates: By clicking the Review Activity Instance Updates button, the user can open a table that dis-plays all instances that were updated and require the user's decision to accept or decline. Users can review them and take action in a batch. Note that the table does not show instances that have been retired.

DataSupplierTable.general: Default data suppliers are defined here. Click the + button to add a new supplier. Only suppliers in the Final state are available for use at the study level. To retire a supplier, use the Inactivate option in the left context menu (the three vertical dots to the left of the supplier row) then the supplier state will be changed to Retired. Retired suppliers may be reactivated using the Reactivate option in the left context menu.

DataSupplierForm.name: Please enter a unique name for the data supplier. Note that the supplier you add must be assigned to a single data type, which will be used as the default.
DataSupplierForm.supplier_type: Data types are defined according to the sponsor-defined code list 'Data Supplier Type'. One supplier can be assigned to one (default) data type.
DataSupplierForm.description: Use this field for any extra descriptive notes about the supplier (e.g., former name).
DataSupplierForm.order: This is a sequence number indicating the supplier's position in the dedicated pull-down list. By default, the field is auto-assigned with the lowest available integer.
DataSupplierForm.order_hint: Leave empty to auto-assign, or enter specific order to insert at that position
DataSupplierForm.api_base_url: Provide API base URL if known.
DataSupplierForm.ui_base_url: Provide UI base URL if known.
DataSupplierForm.origin_source: Clinical Data Sponsor - data recorder by sponsor, for example derived data; Investigator - data reported by the investigator; Study Subject - data reported by the participant; Vendor - data reported by the Vendor.
DataSupplierForm.origin_type: Assigned Value - data that is either: determined by individual judgment as provided by an evaluator, or Coded terms supplied as part of a coding process, or Values set independently of any subject-related data value in order to complete a dataset; Collected Value - a value that is actually observed and recorded by a person or obtained by an instrument. Note that a collected entry translated to a synonymous controlled term still has a type Collected; Copied value - value that is directly transferred from one source to another, possibly with minor formatting or renaming, but without significant calculation or derivation. It maintains its original meaning and allows for traceability; Derived value - a value that is calculated by an algorithm or reproducible rule, and which is dependent upon other data values, including data values available within the dataset or externally provided data values. MethodDef must be used to document the algorithm or rule used for a derived value; Not available - used when the origin is not available and cannot be determined. Sponsors should specify additional details that may be helpful to the reviewer in the Comments section of the data definition file; Other - use this option whenever any other available option is not suitable.

Library.dashboard_description: A simple graphical presentation of the current status and historical trends of the metadata stored in the Library
Library.codelist_description: Find the CDISC controlled terminology, including the code lists, the valid values and associated definitions for each code list and the evolution in packages and terms over time. Sponsor defined list and terms are also included.
Library.dictionaries_description: Find relevant external dictionaries and thesaurus for clinical development such as SNOMED, MedDRA, MED-RT, UNII, LOINC, UCUM. Since some of these dictionaries are very large, only selected terms relevant for the Novo Nordisk pipeline are included.
Library.concepts_description: Find controlled listings of the terms to use for generic concepts such as units, activities, compounds and CRFs.
Library.syntax_templates_description: View (or manage) the different syntax templates used for specifying the objectives, the endpoints, the eligibility, randomisation, dosing and other criteria for the individual studies. Only templates in status Final can be used under Studies.
Library.template_instantiations_description: See how the different templates are used in the studies, including number of studies using a specific syntax and which studies.
Library.template_collections_description: Find relevant template collections for project templates, shared templates and supporting templates.
Library.data_exchange_standards_description: See the complete master model for the following CDISC data exchange standards: CDASH, SDTM and ADaM.
Library.list_description: Find listings of all codes list and terms for controlled terminology, dictionaries, concepts as well as the data exchange standards, in both new and legacy format.
Library.process_overview_description: Find schematic overviews of the activities covered under the Library. You can use these to navigate to the relevant pages to enter or look up information.

ActivityInstanceForm.required_for_activity_help: This flag is used in the standards governance process. The flag is used when some or all instances under an activity is required to be selected when that activity is selected, as an example in questionnaires
ActivityInstanceForm.data_sharing_help: The purpose of this flag is to enable sharing details of the instance with CDISC and other pharmaceutical companies. The flag should not be checked for activity instances which contains Novo Nordisk specific information. For e.g. but not limited to, product information or antibodies specific to products etc.
ActivityInstanceForm.default_selected_help: The purpose of the default flag is to help the user select instances under an activity faster by flagging the instance that is used the most across all studies as the default. This is only used when more than one instance exists under an activity. The user can manually change the selection based on the need of the study
ActivityInstanceForm.data_from_research_lab_help: This flag is used to indicate that the data collection is based on a research lab where all GxP requirements are not necessarily met. This means that the data can´t be used for endpoint analysis
